title: Routing

headings.text: How a Packet Finds Its Path

overview: Routing is the process that directs traffic between different IP networks, ensuring packets find their way from a source subnet to a destination subnet—no matter how many hops or intermediate routers lie in between. In essence, routers build and consult a routing table to make forwarding decisions, much like a GPS chooses the best roads to reach a destinatio

workflow: Identify the user, host, process, network flow, or identity object involved.
